title: INTERPOL Internship 2026 in France (Paid Legal Internship)
summary: Internship 1706: Key Details Organization: INTERPOL Internship Title: Institutional Affairs (IAF) Internship Location: Lyon, France Duration: 6 months Start Date: May 4, 2026 Application Deadline: March 25, 2026 Number of Positions: 2 Open to: Nationals of all INTERPOL member countries (196 countries worldwide) Monthly Stipend: EUR 700 Additional Benefits: Transport subsidy: EUR 30/month Housing allowance: EUR 170/month (eligible interns) Health insurance coverage available INTERPOL is the world’s largest international police organization, with 196 Member Countries. Created in 1923, it facilitates cross-border police co-operation, and supports and assists all organizations, authorities, and services whose mission is to prevent or combat international crime. Title: Internship IAF Directorate: Institutional Affairs Sub-Directorate: Duty station: Lyon Duration (in months): 6.00 Estimated starting date: 4 May 2026 Level of security clearance: Basic Deadline for applications: 25 March 2026 INTERPOL’s Internship Programme welcomes candidates from any of our 196 member countries. INTERPOL actively encourages applications from women and nationals of member countries that are currently unrepresented among our staff ( please click on this link to access the list of countries ). Candidates from these countries are particularly encouraged to apply. Below is the complete list of INTERPOL member countries: A–F Afghanistan Albania Algeria Andorra Angola Antigua and Barbuda Argentina Armenia Australia Austria Azerbaijan Bahamas Bahrain Bangladesh Barbados Belarus Belgium Belize Benin Bhutan Bolivia Bosnia and Herzegovina Botswana Brazil Brunei Bulgaria Burkina Faso Burundi Cabo Verde Cambodia Cameroon Canada Central African Republic Chad Chile China Colombia Comoros Congo (Republic of the) Costa Rica Côte d’Ivoire Croatia Cuba Cyprus Czech Republic Democratic Republic of the Congo Denmark Djibouti Dominica Dominican Republic Ecuador Egypt El Salvador Equatorial Guinea Eritrea Estonia Eswatini Ethiopia Fiji Finland France G–L Gabon Gambia Georgia Germany Ghana Greece Grenada Guatemala Guinea Guinea-Bissau Guyana Haiti Honduras Hungary Iceland India Indonesia Iran Iraq Ireland Israel Italy Jamaica Japan Jordan Kazakhstan Kenya Kiribati Kuwait Kyrgyzstan Laos Latvia Lebanon Lesotho Liberia Libya Liechtenstein Lithuania Luxembourg M–R Madagascar Malawi Malaysia Maldives Mali Malta Marshall Islands Mauritania Mauritius Mexico Micronesia Moldova Monaco Mongolia Montenegro Morocco Mozambique Myanmar Namibia Nauru Nepal Netherlands New Zealand Nicaragua Niger Nigeria North Korea North Macedonia Norway Oman Pakistan Palau Panama Papua New Guinea Paraguay Peru Philippines Poland Portugal Qatar Romania Russia Rwanda S–Z Saint Kitts and Nevis Saint Lucia Saint Vincent and the Grenadines Samoa San Marino São Tomé and Príncipe Saudi Arabia Senegal Serbia Seychelles Sierra Leone Singapore Slovakia Slovenia Solomon Islands Somalia South Africa South Korea South Sudan Spain Sri Lanka Sudan Suriname Sweden Switzerland Syria Tajikistan Tanzania Thailand Timor-Leste Togo Tonga Trinidad and Tobago Tunisia Turkey Turkmenistan Tuvalu Uganda Ukraine United Arab Emirates United Kingdom United States Uruguay Uzbekistan Vanuatu Vatican City Venezuela Vietnam Yemen Zambia Zimbabwe INTERPOL’s recruitment process is merit-based hence all hiring decisions are made considering the applicant’s qualifications and the needs of the Organization. The Programme attracts talented students or recent graduates to the Organization and provides them with: A better understanding of the Organization’s goals and objectives and how international police cooperation is organized An insight into how an international organization operates A framework to enhance their educational experience through practical work assignments, thereby developing skills which are useful for their career and future employment Experience of working in a multicultural, multidisciplinary and multi-ethnic environment, contributing to the development of mutual understanding, trust and tolerance All the relevant information about our Internship Programme may be found on our official web site. As of 1 January 2025, the internship allowance is EUR 700 and a transport subsidy of EUR 30 per month. An additional housing subsidy of EUR 170 per month will be provided to eligible interns. Interns are required to be subscribed to a health insurance coverage for the entire duration of the internship. However, they are eligible to request enrolment in the Organization’s health insurance coverage at no additional cost. Please note INTERPOL does not establish or endorse internship agreements with universities or academic institutions. Internship arrangements and communication are made solely between INTERPOL and the individual selected for the internship. Candidates are encouraged to familiarize themselves with the conditions of internship and the requirements prior to applying for this internship. OVERVIEW The Executive Director Legal Affairs (EDLA) assists the Organization in achieving its aims by ensuring that all its activities and programmes are conducted in accordance with INTERPOL’s legal framework. The core mission of EDLA consists in the provision of substantive and impartial legal advice to the Secretary General, to the bodies of the Organization and to other Executive Directorates of the General Secretariat, in order to promote and develop the rule of law in all aspects of INTERPOL’s activities. EDLA defends INTERPOL against claims arising from its activities and programmes. Within the Executive Directorate Legal Affairs, the Institutional Affairs Directorate (IAF) supports the Organization through the provision of legal services on various aspects of its governance and internal administration. The pillars of the IAF Directorate’s activities are: 1. Institutions and Norms, Secretariat to governing bodies: which includes activities such as interpreting internal legislation and decisions relating to INTERPOL’s Constitution, implementing rules of procedure of policy-making organs and ensuring compliance with public international law and legal standards of international organizations; 2. Privileges Immunities, Relations with Host States, External Relations: which includes activities such as preparing, drafting and/or negotiating agreements with Member Countries related to privileges and immunities, assisting prospective Members with their application to join and implementing procedures for these applications, and ensuring the protection of INTERPOL’s distinctive signs; and 3. Employment law : which includes activities such as providing daily legal services to the Human Resources Management Directorate on the implementation of a Human Resources strategy, and defending the Organization’s interests in the context of staff disputes before INTERPOL’s internal dispute mechanisms and the Administrative Tribunal of the International Labour Organization. To support the Institutional Affairs Directorate, the selected interns will: ▪ Assist in drafting legal deliverables (legal note, legal analysis, memorandum, report or draft legal agreement); ▪ Assist in doing research; ▪ Carry out any other duty assigned by the supervisor. This call for internship is aimed at filling 2 vacant internship positions in IAF. REQUIREMENTS ▪ Be a national of one of the INTERPOL’s 196 member countries. ▪ Have attained the legal age of majority in the country of nationality Training/Education Required ▪ Be enrolled in a, or have graduated from, a law Degree (L.LB., J.D, or an equivalent degree) within the past one year on the date of submission of their application. ▪ Membership of a legal bar of one of INTERPOL’s member countries will be an asset. ▪ Studies of international criminal law will be an asset. Specific Skills ▪ Working knowledge of Microsoft Office tools ▪ Discretion, diplomacy, tact and rigor Languages ▪ Professional fluency in English is required. ▪ Working knowledge of any other INTERPOL official working language (French, Arabic or Spanish) will be considered an asset. APPLICATION PROCESS AND ASSESSMENT METHOD Application Process Only applications submitted in response to a specific internship vacancy will be considered. Due to the large number of applications received, we are unable to answer individual queries. A completed online application is required and should include a cover letter including: ▪ Your motivation to join the INTERPOL internship programme ▪ Your interest in and your assets for this specific internship opportunity ▪ Your expectations for this internship APPLY Assessment Method All applications will be assessed against the requirements indicated in this call for internship. Applicants who meet these requirements most closely will be contacted by the hiring manager directly for further consideration and assessment. INTERPOL does not discriminate on any grounds and candidates will be treated under conditions of equality. Security Clearance A confidentiality vetting inquiry will be carried out on behalf of the Organization prior to confirming any selection. By accepting this offer, you give the Organization expressed consent for completing this inquiry. If selected, you will be required to produce a certificate of non-criminal records (including spent and unspent sentences pronounced by justice) from your country of nationality and from any other country in which you have resided for more than 12 months in the past five years. If you are considered at a further stage of the selection process (interview) we advise you to familiarize yourself with the procedure for requesting such documentation in the concerned countries so that, if accepted, your arrival to the Organization is not unnecessarily delayed. APPLY Find similar jobs: Type of contracts, Internship, European Region, Corporate Posts The post INTERPOL Internship 2026 in France (Paid Legal Internship) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at Makerere University: Fully-funded Undergraduate and Graduate Scholarships for 2026/2027(Open to Africans)
summary: Overview Makerere University, in partnership with the Mastercard Foundation, is offering 1,000 scholarships over the next 10 years to academically talented young men and women from Sub-Saharan Africa who face financial hardship. These scholarships aim to provide access to higher education at both the undergraduate and graduate levels. Undergraduate Scholarships About the Scholarship Makerere University Management and the Mastercard Foundation renewed their partnership for the second phase of the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program through 2034. The Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at Makerere University aims to deepen inclusive University education and empower 1,000 young people from Africa to make meaningful contributions to national and regional development. The Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at Makerere University will provide scholarships for 10 years at the main campus for Undergraduate (Bachelor s degree) and Graduate levels. The Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program will support talented young men and women from Sub-Saharan Africa who are facing economic barriers in pursuing university education. Makerere University is pleased to announce the Second Cohort of [245] Undergraduate and [13] Master s scholarships through the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program for students joining Makerere University s main campus in the Academic Year 2026/2027 under the Private Sponsorship Scheme. Application Procedure Prospective applicants for the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at Makerere University s main campus can access and complete the application form AT NO COST through the Online Application Portal https://apply.mastercardfoundation.mak.ac.ug/scholarships Deadline for Submission of Application The application submission deadline is 5 th June 2026, at 11:59 PM EAT. For inquiries or clarifications, please call our office mobile line +256 700-716-128 or email info@mastercardfdn.mak.ac.ug Eligibility To be eligible to apply for the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at Makerere University s main campus, an applicant must belong to one of the following categories: Ugandan nationals, refugees, students with disabilities, internally displaced young people, or international students, and fulfil the following conditions; The applicant must have applied for admission to Makerere University s main campus under the private sponsorship scheme for the academic year 2025/2026. The Applicant must have either; Completed their advanced secondary level education (or its equivalent) in an African country or Hold second class/credit diploma from any recognized institution or Must have passed the mature age entry of Makerere University The Applicant should be experiencing financial hardships. The Applicant should not be a holder of any other scholarship, including the Government of Uganda Scholarship. The Applicant should not have completed any undergraduate degree Programme. The Applicant should have demonstrated leadership potential and/or community service. The Applicant should be 28 years old or younger at the time of the application deadline. Applicants in the Refugee or Persons with Disabilities categories should be 32 years old or younger at the time of the application deadline. Only shortlisted applicants will be contacted for further selection engagements. Applicants are advised to read the application guidelines before completing the form. NOTE The Department of Academic Registrar is responsible for all University admissions. All scholarship applicants should first apply for admission to Makerere University s main campus through the private sponsorship scheme . At least 70% of the scholarships awarded through the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program will be awarded to young women while a minimum of 30% to young men. Young women are therefore encouraged to apply. At least 25% of the scholarships awarded through the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program shall be awarded to Refugees and Internally Displaced Persons (IDPs). Young people in this Category are therefore encouraged to apply. Internally displaced people , referred to as IDPs, are persons who have been forced to flee their homes due to conflict, violence, persecution, or natural disasters. These individuals are often resettled in other areas within the borders of their own country. They should have a recommendation from the Office of the Prime Minister or its representative in the area . At least 10% of the scholarships awarded through the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program shall be awarded to Young People with Disabilities. Young people in this category are therefore encouraged to apply Incomplete application forms will be automatically disqualified. The Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at Makerere University practices merit principles and has zero tolerance for dishonesty. Any form of influence peddling will automatically disqualify the applicant. Cases of impersonation, falsification of documents, or giving false/incomplete information, whether discovered at the time of receiving the award or afterwards, will lead to automatic cancellation of the award, refund of the money already spent on a Mastercard Foundation Scholar, and prosecution in the courts of law of Uganda if necessary. Makerere University appeals to the public to be aware of fraudsters within or outside of the University who might want to take advantage of the scholarship application process by soliciting money with a promise of securing a scholarship for an applicant. THE APPLICATION PROCESS OF THE MASTERCARD FOUNDATION SCHOLARS PROGRAM AT MAKERERE UNIVERSITY MAIN CAMPUS IS FREE OF CHARGE ; individuals caught in the act of conning the public will be prosecuted in courts of law. Only successful applicants will be notified at every stage of the application process. Those applicants who will not have heard from us by the end of August 2026 should consider their application unsuccessful. Filling out and applying the form does not guarantee acceptance into the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at Makerere University s main campus. Ready to Apply? Click here to get started . Graduate (Master’s) Scholarships Scholarships for Masters Programmes 2026/2027 About the Scholarship Makerere University Management and the Mastercard Foundation renewed their partnership for the second phase of the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program through 2034. The Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at Makerere University aims to deepen inclusive University education and empower 1,000 young people from Africa to make meaningful contributions to national and regional development. The Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at Makerere University will provide scholarships for 10 years at the main campus for Undergraduate (Bachelor s degree) and Graduate levels. The Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program will support talented young men and women from Sub-Saharan Africa who are facing economic barriers in pursuing university education. Makerere University is pleased to announce the Second Cohort of [245] Undergraduate and [13] Master s scholarships through the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program for students joining Makerere University s main campus in the Academic Year under the Private Sponsorship Scheme. Application Procedure Prospective applicants for the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at Makerere University s main campus can access and complete the application form AT NO COST through the Online Application Portal [https://apply.mastercardfoundation.mak.ac.ug/]. Deadline for Submission of Application The application submission deadline is 5 th June 2026, at 11:59 PM EAT. For inquiries or clarifications, please call our office mobile line +256 700-716-128 or email info@mastercardfdn.mak.ac.ug Eligibility To be eligible to apply for the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at Makerere University s main campus, an applicant must belong to one of the following categories: Ugandan nationals, refugees, students with disabilities, internally displaced young people, or international students and fulfill the following conditions. The applicant must have applied for admission to Makerere University s main campus for the academic year The Applicant must have completed their undergraduate education in an African country. The Applicant should be experiencing financial hardships. The Applicant should not be a holder of any other scholarship. The Applicant should not be pursuing or have completed any Master’s degree studies. The applicant should have demonstrated leadership potential and/ or community service. The Applicant should not be over 35 years of age at the application deadline. Applicants in the Refugee and Students with Disabilities categories should be under 40 years of age at the application deadline. A refugee applicant MUST hold a Refugee Identity card or Family Attestation. The applicant should have applied for any degree programs prioritized under the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program (please refer to the application form for a list of prioritized programs ). Only shortlisted applicants will be contacted for further selection engagements. Applicants are advised to read the application guidelines before completing the form. NOTE The Department of Academic Registrar is responsible for all University admissions. All Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program scholarship applicants should first apply for admission to Makerere University s main campus . At least 70% of the scholarships awarded through the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program will be awarded to young women while a minimum of 30% to young men. Young women are therefore encouraged to apply At least 25% of the scholarships awarded through the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program shall be awarded to Refugees and Internally Displaced Persons (IDPs). Young people in this Category are therefore encouraged to apply. Internally displaced people , referred to as IDPs, are persons who have been forced to flee their homes due to conflict, violence, persecution, or natural disasters. These individuals are often resettled in other areas within the borders of their own country. They should have a recommendation from the Office of the Prime Minister. At least 10% of the scholarships awarded through the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program shall be awarded to Young People with Disabilities. Young people in this category are therefore encouraged to apply Incomplete application forms will be automatically disqualified. The Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at Makerere University practices merit principles and has zero tolerance for dishonesty. Any form of influence peddling will automatically disqualify the applicant. Cases of impersonation, falsification of documents, or giving false/incomplete information, whether discovered at the time of receiving the award or afterwards, will lead to automatic cancellation of the award, refund of the money already spent on a Mastercard Foundation Scholar, and prosecution in the courts of law of Uganda if necessary. Makerere University appeals to the public to be aware of fraudsters within or outside of the University who might want to take advantage of the scholarship application process by soliciting money with a promise of securing a scholarship for an applicant. THE APPLICATION PROCESS OF THE MASTERCARD FOUNDATION SCHOLARS PROGRAM AT MAKERERE UNIVERSITY MAIN CAMPUS IS FREE OF CHARGE ; individuals caught in the act of conning the public will be prosecuted in courts of law. Only successful applicants will be notified at every stage of the scholarship application process. Those applicants who will not have heard from us by the end of August 2026 should consider their application unsuccessful. Filling out an application form DOES NOT guarantee a Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program scholarship at Makerere University s main campus. Ready to Apply? Click here to get Started . title: Fully Funded 2026 ACES Political Economy Summer School in Nepal (Global Opportunity for PhD Students)
summary: If you are a PhD student in economics or related fields seeking an elite international academic experience, the 2026 ACES Political Economy Summer School offers a rare opportunity to engage with world-class scholars, receive personalized mentorship, and expand your research network—all in a fully supported academic environment. About the ACES Political Economy Summer School The ACES Summer School in Political Economy is a highly competitive, multi-day academic program designed to train and mentor emerging scholars in key areas of economics. This intensive 3-day workshop brings together leading researchers and promising PhD students to explore cutting-edge topics in: Political Economy Economic History Development Economics Participants benefit from a structured academic environment that combines: Advanced lectures by globally recognized faculty Interactive mentoring sessions Research feedback workshops Academic networking opportunities The program is specifically designed to accelerate academic growth and research quality by exposing participants to frontier research and critical discussions in economics. Why You Should Apply This summer school is not just another academic event—it is a career-shaping opportunity. Here is what makes it exceptional: 1. Direct Mentorship from World-Class Economists Participants interact closely with renowned faculty from top global institutions such as: Yale University Stanford University University of Chicago London School of Economics These experts provide personalized feedback on your research ideas, helping refine your academic direction. 2. Exposure to Cutting-Edge Research The program focuses on current and impactful research trends, ensuring participants stay ahead in their academic careers. You will explore real-world economic challenges and advanced theoretical frameworks. 3. Strong Academic Networking You will join a global cohort of PhD students and researchers, creating long-term collaborations and professional connections across continents. 4. Financial Support Free participation (no tuition or application fee) Accommodation provided At least one meal per day covered While travel funding is limited, priority support may be available for students from low-income countries. Who Can Apply? Eligible Category of Applicants This opportunity is specifically designed for: PhD students Fields of Study Applicants must have academic or research interest in: Political Economy Development Economics Economic History Eligible Countries and Regions Open to applicants worldwide Strong encouragement for candidates from: Developing countries Low-income regions The program promotes global diversity and inclusion, making it accessible to students from Africa, Asia, Latin America, Europe, and beyond. Key Qualifications and Requirements To be considered for the ACES Summer School 2026, applicants must: Be currently enrolled in a PhD program Demonstrate strong interest in political economy or related fields Submit a 2-page research outline on a specific topic Provide an updated Curriculum Vitae (CV) Your research proposal is critical—it should clearly outline your research question, methodology, and contribution to the field. What You Will Gain from the Program Participating in this summer school will provide: Academic Benefits Deep understanding of modern economic research Structured feedback to improve your dissertation or research work Exposure to interdisciplinary approaches Career Benefits Connections with top economists and institutions Increased visibility in the academic community Potential future collaborations and recommendations Personal Development Enhanced critical thinking Improved research communication skills Global perspective on economic issues Program Structure The 3-day program is designed to maximize learning and engagement: Day 1–3 Highlights Faculty-led lectures Research presentations by participants One-on-one mentoring sessions Peer discussions and feedback Networking events This format ensures a balanced mix of theory, practice, and interaction. How to Apply To apply for the ACES Summer School 2026: Required Documents Two-page research outline Curriculum Vitae (CV) Submission Method Submit your documents via email to the program coordinator (as indicated on the official page) Apply here Important Notes There is no guarantee of travel funding, so applicants should explore alternative funding sources. The program is highly competitive, with limited slots available. Strong research proposals significantly improve your chances of selection. Deadline 15th April 2026 (11:59 PM EST) The ACES Political Economy Summer School 2026 is a prestigious academic opportunity that can significantly elevate your research career. With access to top-tier mentorship, global networking, and advanced research exposure, this program is ideal for ambitious PhD students aiming to make an impact in economics. title: CIVICUS Call for Applications: Social Listening and Digital Manipulation 12 month Research Opportunity
summary: The CIVICUS social listening and digital manipulation opportunity offers researchers and civil society actors a chance to contribute to critical global work on understanding online disinformation, civic space, and digital threats. This call invites individuals with expertise in digital analysis and research to support efforts aimed at strengthening democratic participation and countering harmful online practices. About the Program This opportunity is part of ongoing efforts by CIVICUS to monitor and respond to challenges affecting civic space worldwide. CIVICUS works across more than 190 countries to strengthen citizen action and protect fundamental freedoms, including freedom of expression and access to information. The Terms of Reference outline a role focused on social listening and analysis of digital manipulation trends. This includes monitoring online conversations, identifying coordinated disinformation campaigns, and analyzing how narratives spread across digital platforms. The selected participant(s) will contribute to research that helps civil society organizations better understand how digital environments are being shaped—and sometimes distorted—by various actors. This includes examining tactics such as misinformation, algorithmic amplification, coordinated messaging, and online harassment. Digital technologies have transformed civic engagement, enabling new forms of participation but also introducing risks such as censorship, surveillance, and manipulation. CIVICUS Monitor findings highlight that digital repression—including disinformation campaigns and coordinated manipulation—is increasingly affecting civic space globally. Why This Opportunity Matters The rise of digital manipulation has become a major concern for civil society, governments, and researchers alike. Online platforms are now central to how people access information, organize movements, and engage in public debate. However, these same platforms are also used to spread misleading narratives and suppress certain voices. Social listening plays a crucial role in identifying these patterns. By analyzing online conversations and trends, researchers can detect harmful narratives early and support efforts to counter them effectively. This opportunity is particularly important in the context of elections, social movements, and public policy discussions, where misinformation and manipulation can significantly influence outcomes. Research shows that tactics such as coordinated disinformation, censorship, and online harassment are increasingly used to limit civic participation and distort public discourse. By contributing to this work, participants help strengthen transparency, accountability, and resilience in digital spaces. What Participants Will Gain Participants will gain valuable experience working at the intersection of digital technology, research, and civic engagement. This includes developing advanced skills in social listening, data analysis, and identifying patterns of digital manipulation. They will also contribute to impactful global research that informs civil society strategies and policy discussions. This exposure can enhance professional credibility and open opportunities in fields such as digital governance, policy research, and media analysis. Another key benefit is the opportunity to work with an internationally recognized civil society network. Participants will engage with experts, practitioners, and organizations working on issues related to democracy, human rights, and digital transformation. In addition, participants will deepen their understanding of how digital ecosystems function, including how algorithms, coordinated behavior, and content dynamics influence public opinion and civic participation. Who Can Apply This opportunity is suitable for individuals with experience or strong interest in digital research, social media analysis, or civic space monitoring. It is particularly relevant for researchers, analysts, and professionals working in areas such as media, communications, public policy, and human rights. Applicants should demonstrate analytical skills, familiarity with digital platforms, and an understanding of issues related to disinformation and online manipulation. Application Process and Deadline Interested applicants should review the full Terms of Reference and submit their application as instructed on the official CIVICUS page . The process typically involves providing relevant professional information, demonstrating experience in research or digital analysis, and outlining suitability for the role. Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible and ensure that all required materials are submitted in line with the specified guidelines on the official page. Application Deadline: 30 March 2026 Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. title: University of Calgary International Entrance Scholarship: Study in Canada with $20,000 Funding
summary: The University of Calgary International Entrance Scholarship offers international students a prestigious opportunity to study in Canada while receiving significant financial support. Valued at $20,000 and renewable for up to four years, this scholarship recognizes academic excellence and helps reduce the cost of undergraduate education. About the Program The University of Calgary International Entrance Scholarship is one of the institution’s prestigious entrance awards offered annually to international undergraduate students. It is available to students entering any undergraduate degree program in the fall term, provided they meet the university’s admission and English language requirements. The scholarship is valued at $20,000 per year and is renewable in the second, third, and fourth years of study. Renewal is contingent upon maintaining a minimum GPA of 2.60 over at least 24 units in the previous academic year, as well as continuing to pay international tuition fees. This award is part of the university’s effort to attract talented students from around the world and support them throughout their academic journey. With only a limited number of scholarships available each year, it remains highly competitive and prestigious. Why This Opportunity Matters Studying abroad can be financially challenging, especially for international students. Scholarships like this play a crucial role in making high-quality education more accessible by significantly reducing tuition costs. The University of Calgary International Entrance Scholarship is particularly valuable because it is renewable. This means students who maintain strong academic performance can receive continued financial support throughout their degree, potentially totaling up to $80,000 over four years. Beyond financial benefits, receiving a merit-based scholarship enhances a student’s academic profile. It signals excellence, discipline, and commitment—qualities that are highly valued by employers and academic institutions alike. Additionally, studying at a globally recognized university provides students with access to quality education, diverse learning environments, and international career opportunities. What Participants Will Gain Recipients of this scholarship gain more than just financial support. One of the most immediate benefits is the reduction in tuition costs, allowing students to focus more on their studies and less on financial pressures. Students will also benefit from studying at a leading Canadian university, gaining access to high-quality academic programs across a wide range of disciplines. This exposure helps build strong academic foundations and prepares students for global career opportunities. Another key benefit is the recognition that comes with receiving a prestigious scholarship. This distinction can strengthen future applications for jobs, graduate programs, or additional funding opportunities. The renewable nature of the award further encourages academic consistency and performance, helping students stay motivated throughout their studies. Who Can Apply The scholarship is open to international students who are entering their first year of an undergraduate program at the University of Calgary. Applicants must not be Canadian citizens or permanent residents and must be required to pay international tuition fees. Candidates must demonstrate strong academic achievement and meet the university’s English language proficiency requirements. The scholarship is awarded based on academic merit, making it highly competitive. Eligible Areas The scholarship is open to students from all countries. As long as applicants meet the eligibility requirements and are classified as international students, they can apply regardless of their country of origin. Application Process and Deadline To apply for the University of Calgary International Entrance Scholarship, students must first apply for admission to an undergraduate program at the university. After submitting their application, they can access the scholarship application through the university’s student portal under High School Prestige Awards. The application deadline for the scholarship is December 1 . Early preparation is essential, as applicants need to complete both their admission and scholarship applications within this timeframe. Learn more about the University of Calgary International Entrance Scholarship on the University of Calgary website. title: Apply Now: Road to Research Scholarship 2026 at Macquarie University
summary: Are you looking to begin your research journey in Australia? The Road to Research Scholarship at Macquarie University offers a valuable opportunity for students to undertake a Master of Research (MRes) while receiving financial support to build strong foundations for a future research career. This scholarship is designed for students who are ready to develop their research skills through a structured program that combines coursework and independent research. It provides a pathway for those considering further academic study, including progression to a PhD. About the Road to Research Scholarship The Road to Research (R2R) Scholarship supports students enrolling in the Master of Research (MRes) program at Macquarie University in Sydney. The MRes is a specialized postgraduate degree that prepares students for advanced research by combining academic training with hands-on research experience. The scholarship is available for both domestic and international applicants , provided they are onshore in Australia at the time of application and able to study full-time in Sydney. The program is particularly suited for students who want to gain research experience before committing to a longer doctoral program, making it an ideal stepping stone toward a PhD or research-focused career. Why This Opportunity Matters Starting a research career can often feel inaccessible due to financial barriers and limited opportunities for early-stage researchers. Programs like the Road to Research Scholarship help bridge that gap by providing structured, funded entry points into research. This scholarship is particularly important because it supports students at a critical stage—when they are transitioning from coursework-based study to independent research. By offering financial support and access to academic supervision, the program enables students to develop essential research skills, build confidence, and explore their academic interests in depth. In a world increasingly driven by innovation and evidence-based solutions, research plays a crucial role in addressing global challenges—from climate change to health and technology. Supporting emerging researchers is key to ensuring that new ideas and discoveries continue to shape the future. For students considering a long-term academic or research career, this scholarship provides both the training and support needed to take that first step . What the Scholarship Covers The Road to Research Scholarship provides financial support to help students focus on their studies and research. Successful applicants will receive: A tuition fee offset for the duration of the program (up to one year) A living stipend of approximately AUD $39,700 per year This funding allows students to dedicate themselves fully to their research training while studying in one of Australia’s leading research environments. Who Can Apply The scholarship is open to students who meet the eligibility requirements for the Master of Research program and are prepared to begin their studies in Session 2 (July) 2026 . Applicants must: Be eligible for admission into the Master of Research program Meet English language requirements Be enrolled as a full-time student Be onshore in Australia at the time of application Intend to live in Sydney during the program Have (or be able to secure) academic supervision for their research project International applicants must also hold a valid visa that allows them to study full-time in Australia. Selection is highly competitive and based primarily on academic merit , typically measured through GPA or equivalent qualifications. Application Process and Selection Applicants must submit a complete application, including all required documents, before the deadline. Incomplete applications will not be considered. Selection is highly competitive and based primarily on academic merit. Macquarie University evaluates candidates using their academic results and, where applicable, assesses international qualifications against Australian standards. Application Deadline: 30 April 2026 (11:00 PM AEST) Program Start: July 2026 (Session 2) Location: Sydney, Australia Applicants are encouraged to apply early to ensure all documentation is submitted correctly and on time. Find full details and apply on the official Macquarie University website. title: e-MOVE Free Agribusiness e-Learning Courses: Build Skills in Cashew Value Chains and Entrepreneurship
summary: The e-MOVE free agribusiness e-learning courses are designed for individuals seeking to strengthen their knowledge in agriculture, entrepreneurship, and value chain development. Through flexible online learning, participants gain access to industry-relevant training that equips them with the skills needed to succeed in agribusiness and related sectors. About the Program The e-MOVE Programme, part of the Market Oriented Value Chains for Jobs and Growth (MOVE-ComCashew) project, delivers free online courses focused on agribusiness development and the cashew value chain. The initiative is supported by development partners, including German Cooperation (GIZ) and the European Union, with a strong emphasis on improving skills, innovation, and market access in the agricultural sector. The courses are designed using internationally recognized tools and insights drawn from years of practical experience in agribusiness and value chain development. They aim to support stakeholders across the entire cashew value chain, from production to processing and marketing. Participants access the courses through a digital learning platform, where they can engage with structured modules, complete knowledge assessments, and track their progress. The program also fosters a learning community committed to advancing agribusiness and strengthening value chains, particularly in regions where agriculture plays a critical economic role. Why This Opportunity Matters Agribusiness remains a key driver of economic growth and employment, particularly in developing regions. However, many young people and small-scale entrepreneurs face challenges in accessing relevant training and market-oriented skills. The e-MOVE Programme addresses this gap by offering free, accessible education tailored to real-world agricultural value chains. By focusing on the cashew sector, the program provides targeted knowledge that can directly improve productivity, quality, and market competitiveness. This opportunity is especially important for individuals seeking to build sustainable livelihoods in agriculture. It supports not only technical skills but also entrepreneurial thinking, enabling participants to identify business opportunities and respond to market demands. Additionally, the flexibility of online learning ensures that participants can engage with the material regardless of their location or schedule, making it an inclusive and practical solution for continuous learning. What Participants Will Gain Participants in the e-MOVE courses will gain a combination of technical knowledge and entrepreneurial skills that are directly applicable to agribusiness activities. They will develop a deeper understanding of cashew production, including farm management practices that can improve yield and efficiency. The courses also cover processing techniques and quality improvement, which are essential for adding value and meeting market standards. In addition, learners will acquire skills in marketing and value chain development, helping them better understand how agricultural products move from producers to markets. This knowledge is critical for increasing profitability and expanding business opportunities. The program also emphasizes agribusiness planning and entrepreneurship, equipping participants with the tools needed to start or grow their own ventures. Another key benefit is the opportunity to earn certificates upon completing the courses. These certificates validate participants’ knowledge and can enhance their professional credibility in the agricultural sector. Who Can Apply The e-MOVE Programme is open to a diverse group of participants interested in agribusiness and agricultural development. It is particularly suited for young entrepreneurs and individuals looking to start or grow agribusiness ventures. Farmers and value-chain actors can benefit from the practical knowledge offered in the courses, while agriculture professionals and students can use the program to strengthen their expertise and career prospects. The courses are also suitable for anyone with an interest in agribusiness development, regardless of their prior experience. Eligible Areas The program is open globally, allowing participants from different regions to enroll and benefit from the training. There is particularly strong participation from Africa and other developing regions, where agribusiness and agricultural value chains play a significant role in economic development. Application Process and Deadline Interested individuals can apply by accessing the online learning platform and enrolling in the available courses. The process is straightforward and does not require any application fee. Once enrolled, participants can complete the courses at their own pace, progressing through modules and assessments according to their schedule. Upon successful completion, they receive certificates recognizing their achievement. There is no fixed deadline for application, as the courses are available on a rolling basis. This allows learners to start at any time and engage with the program at their convenience. Learn more and enroll on the official Antingi website . Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. title: Amazing Global Women in Management Leadership Program Gwim 2026
summary: The Empowering Global Women in Management Leadership Program 2026 (GWIM 76) is a prestigious international leadership initiative designed to strengthen the leadership capacity of women professionals and entrepreneurs across the world. Implemented by Counterpart International with support from the ExxonMobil Foundation, the program aims to accelerate women’s economic leadership and empower participants with the skills needed to drive inclusive economic and social transformation in their communities and organizations. The Global Women in Management Program (GWIM) was launched in 2005 and has since become one of the most influential leadership development initiatives supporting women leaders worldwide. Through integrated training, mentorship, executive coaching, and global networking opportunities, the program equips participants with the tools needed to expand their leadership influence and strengthen the institutions and enterprises they represent. The 2026 cohort, known as GWIM 76, offers a hybrid learning model combining a two-week immersive leadership experience in Washington, DC with several months of structured virtual training and mentorship. This approach allows participants to engage deeply with leadership concepts while applying newly acquired skills in real-world professional environments. Applications for the program close on April 30, 2026 at 11:59 PM EST. Leadership Development Through the GWIM Program The GWIM Leadership Program focuses on strengthening women’s leadership and management capacity while addressing systemic barriers that limit women’s economic advancement. The program emphasizes a results-oriented approach that combines academic knowledge, practical leadership training, and collaborative learning. Participants will gain insights into leadership development, economic empowerment strategies, organizational management, and the role of innovation in modern leadership environments. The program also emphasizes how emerging technologies such as artificial intelligence and digital platforms can support economic growth and institutional development. By combining traditional leadership training with modern digital innovation strategies, the program prepares women leaders to navigate rapidly evolving economic and technological environments. Two-Week Leadership Experience in Washington, DC A central component of the program is the two-week in-person leadership training in Washington, DC, scheduled from October 19 to October 30, 2026. During this immersive experience, participants will engage with policymakers, global development experts, academics, and thought leaders. The sessions will include leadership workshops, policy dialogues, collaborative discussions, and strategic leadership exercises designed to strengthen participants’ leadership capabilities. The Washington, DC residency provides participants with valuable exposure to international policy environments and leadership practices. Participants will have the opportunity to learn from experienced professionals who are actively shaping economic and social development initiatives worldwide. This in-person training experience also provides a collaborative environment where participants can exchange ideas, build professional networks, and develop partnerships that extend beyond the duration of the program. Virtual Leadership Learning and Mentorship After completing the Washington, DC training, participants will continue the program through six months of virtual learning, mentorship, and leadership development activities. These virtual sessions will be conducted on a monthly or bi-monthly basis and will total approximately 16 to 20 hours of structured learning. The online component is designed to reinforce the leadership skills developed during the in-person training while providing ongoing mentorship and peer collaboration opportunities. Participants will engage in discussions, leadership exercises, group work, and practical assignments that help them apply new knowledge within their organizations and communities. The program also includes a practicum component, where participants develop action plans for their leadership and professional development. These plans enable participants to translate the lessons learned during the program into measurable outcomes in their professional environments. Focus Areas of the Leadership Program The GWIM program focuses on several key areas that are essential for effective leadership in the modern global economy. Economic Leadership Participants will develop the skills required to influence economic growth and development within their organizations and communities. Training sessions will focus on strategic planning, resource mobilization, and sustainable organizational growth. Artificial Intelligence and Digital Transformation The program integrates emerging technologies such as artificial intelligence and digital platforms into leadership training. Participants will explore how digital transformation can support innovation, productivity, and organizational development. Executive Leadership and Strategic Decision-Making The program strengthens participants’ ability to lead organizations, manage complex challenges, and make strategic decisions. Leadership workshops and coaching sessions help participants develop the confidence and skills needed to lead effectively in dynamic environments. Global Networking Opportunities One of the most valuable aspects of the GWIM program is the opportunity to join a global network of women leaders. Participants will engage with women professionals from different regions and sectors, creating opportunities for collaboration, knowledge sharing, and long-term partnerships. The program fosters meaningful connections among participants and provides access to a global community of GWIM alumnae who continue to support each other’s leadership journeys. Participating Countries The 2026 cohort welcomes women leaders from the following countries: Brazil, Egypt, Guyana, India, Indonesia, Kazakhstan, Mexico, Mozambique, China, Papua New Guinea, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Vietnam, and the United Arab Emirates. By bringing together women from diverse regions, the program promotes cross-cultural learning and global collaboration. Learn More About This Opportunity To learn more about the Empowering Global Women in Management Leadership Program 2026 (GWIM 76) and submit your application, click here : Conclusion The Global Women in Management Leadership Program 2026 offers a unique opportunity for women leaders to strengthen their leadership capacity, expand their professional networks, and gain practical skills in economic leadership and digital transformation. Through its hybrid learning model combining in-person training and virtual mentorship, the program equips participants with the knowledge and tools needed to lead transformational change within their organizations and communities. title: Unlock Global Grants for Africa: Free Funding Opportunities, Networking, and Program Support for Organizations
summary: Introduction: A Gateway to Global Opportunities for Africa Global Grants and Opportunities for Africa (GGA) is an inclusive and far-reaching platform designed to connect African individuals and institutions with credible, high-impact opportunities from across the world. The platform serves a diverse audience, including Civil Society Organizations (CSOs), Non-Governmental Organizations (NGOs), companies, universities, enterprises, startups, scholars, and other development-focused ventures. At its core, GGA functions as a centralized hub where thousands of global funding opportunities, calls for proposals, fellowships, and partnerships are curated and shared. Its primary objective is to ensure that African stakeholders—regardless of size or stage—have access to timely, relevant, and actionable opportunities that align with their missions and goals. All services provided by the platform are completely free of charge. Supported by a team of experienced experts, GGA not only aggregates opportunities but also ensures that users are consistently updated through its communication channels. By following the platform, users gain access to daily updates on newly available grants and programs tailored to various sectors and interests. Commitment to Safety and Transparency One of the defining features of the Global Grants and Opportunities for Africa platform is its strong commitment to protecting users from fraudulent schemes. In a space where grant scams and misleading opportunities are increasingly common, GGA takes deliberate steps to screen and verify all shared content before publication. However, the platform also emphasizes the importance of personal due diligence. Users are strongly advised to independently assess every opportunity they pursue. A key warning sign highlighted by GGA is any application process that requests payment for services. Legitimate grant opportunities do not require applicants to pay fees during the application stage, and users are encouraged to avoid such engagements entirely. This dual approach—platform-level screening combined with user-level vigilance—ensures a safer and more trustworthy experience for all participants. Key Advantages of Joining the Platform Becoming part of the Global Grants and Opportunities for Africa network offers several important benefits: 1. Timely Access to Grants and Calls for Proposals GGA provides real-time updates on newly released grants and funding calls. These opportunities target a wide range of beneficiaries, including African and international CSOs, NGOs, enterprises, and companies. By staying connected to the platform, users can respond quickly to funding opportunities, increasing their chances of success. 2. Networking Opportunities The platform facilitates connections among African scholars, universities, NGOs, and other institutions. When announcements are made, users can engage in collaborative opportunities that extend beyond borders. This networking aspect is particularly valuable for organizations seeking partnerships, knowledge exchange, and joint funding applications. 3. Program Consultation for Growing Ventures In addition to sharing opportunities, GGA offers consultation support for emerging and growing initiatives across Africa. This includes guidance on program development, proposal writing, and strategic positioning. Such technical support is critical for organizations aiming to build resilience and scale their impact. The Driving Philosophy: Addressing Africa’s Development Challenges The foundation of GGA’s work is rooted in the recognition of Africa’s pressing development challenges. With approximately 226.7 million people facing hunger across the continent, there is an urgent need for coordinated action and sustainable solutions. Charities, diaspora communities, NGOs, and companies continue to play a vital role in addressing these challenges. However, many of these actors face limitations in accessing funding, technical expertise, and global networks. GGA seeks to bridge this gap by connecting, strengthening, and empowering a dynamic network of civil society organizations and other stakeholders. Its mission is to contribute to the eradication of poverty, inequality, and injustice by ensuring that those working on the frontlines have the resources and support they need. Vision and Long-Term Impact The platform has an ambitious vision: to engage more than 50,000 like-minded institutions both within and outside Africa. Through this network, GGA aims to indirectly impact up to 50 million people in need. This vision underscores the platform’s belief in collective action. By enabling collaboration and resource mobilization at scale, GGA aspires to create a ripple effect where local initiatives grow into transformative movements with global reach. Scope of Opportunities Available GGA aggregates opportunities for a wide spectrum of stakeholders, including: Individuals seeking scholarships, fellowships, or research funding CSOs and NGOs pursuing grants for community projects Startups and enterprises looking for investment and innovation funding Large organizations and institutions aiming to expand their programs Most of the grants shared on the platform are designed to strengthen organizations at the grassroots level. These grants focus on enhancing the capacity of local actors to mobilize resources, implement effective programs, and serve their communities more efficiently. Some funding opportunities may also have specific geographic or thematic priorities, allowing organizations to align their applications with targeted development goals. Demonstrated Impact: Grantmaking Achievements The effectiveness of the platform and its broader ecosystem can be seen through recent grantmaking data. In the 2023/2024 financial year: A total of 73 grants were awarded 63 community philanthropy organizations benefited 4 individual recipients received support Grants were distributed across 30 countries The total funding disbursed amounted to £1,419,803 These figures highlight the scale and reach of the funding landscape that GGA helps its users access. They also demonstrate the growing importance of community philanthropy as a mechanism for sustainable development. Grant Eligibility: Who Can Apply? The platform features opportunities that are particularly relevant to organizations engaged in community philanthropy. Eligible entities may include: Community philanthropy organizations Community foundations Women’s funds Environmental funds National public foundations Grassroots grantmakers Any organization aligned with the principles of community philanthropy These organizations are typically characterized by their strong connection to the communities they serve and their commitment to locally driven development. Key Areas of Interest for Applicants Organizations interested in applying for grants featured on the platform are often those that: Aim to shift power and decision-making closer to communities Focus on recognizing and strengthening local assets and resources Experiment with innovative governance and resource mobilization approaches Promote community-led development and participation Additionally, applicants are expected to demonstrate a deep understanding of their communities, viewing them as sources of knowledge, relationships, and agency. Organizational Practices and Values Successful applicants typically exhibit the following practices: Use grantmaking as a strategic tool to empower local groups Foster a culture of giving and co-investment within their communities Build trust, networks, and collaborative relationships Address complex or underrepresented issues, even when they are unpopular These values align with the broader goal of creating inclusive, participatory, and sustainable development systems. Geographic Focus While the platform serves a global audience, there is a strong emphasis on organizations based in: Africa Asia The Middle East Central and Eastern Europe Latin America and the Caribbean Priority is often given to regions with limited access to funding opportunities. However, organizations in the Global North that represent disadvantaged communities may also be considered on a case-by-case basis, depending on funding restrictions. Determining Your Fit If your organization meets most of the criteria outlined above, it is likely a strong candidate for the types of opportunities shared through the platform. Even if you are unsure, exploring additional resources—such as educational materials on community philanthropy—can help you better understand your alignment and readiness. Click Here on details on how to apply. Conclusion Global Grants and Opportunities for Africa stands as a vital resource for anyone seeking to access funding, build partnerships, and strengthen development initiatives across the continent. By offering free, curated, and verified opportunities, along with technical support and networking avenues, the platform empowers African stakeholders to take meaningful action. Through its commitment to transparency, inclusivity, and impact, GGA is not only connecting people to opportunities but also contributing to a broader movement aimed at transforming communities and improving lives at scale. Discover more similar grant opportunities Here. The post Unlock Global Grants for Africa: Free Funding Opportunities, Networking, and Program Support for Organizations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Rare Impact Fund Request for Proposals (RFP) 2026: $2.5M+ Grants to Strengthen Youth Mental Health Workforce (Up to $500,000)
summary: The Rare Impact Fund , founded by Selena Gomez, has launched a major Request for Proposals (RFP) 2026 to strengthen the nonclinical youth mental health workforce in the United States. This funding opportunity will provide $250,000 to $500,000 in grants to nonprofit organizations working to expand access to youth mental health support, workforce development, and community-based care systems . If your organization is focused on youth mental health, workforce development, or community-based support systems , this is a significant funding opportunity to scale your impact. About the Rare Impact Fund The Rare Impact Fund was established to address the global youth mental health crisis by increasing access to: Mental health care Stigma-free community support Culturally responsive resources The Fund is mobilizing $100 million and currently supports 30 nonprofit partners across five continents , focusing on: School-based mental health solutions Networks of support Suicide prevention and crisis response The initiative is fiscally sponsored by the Hopewell Fund , a U.S.-based public charity supporting innovative social change projects. What Is the 2026 Funding Opportunity? The Rare Impact Fund is investing $2.5 million+ in organizations that strengthen the nonclinical youth mental health workforce . Grant Details Funding Amount: $250,000 – $500,000 Grant Duration: 2 years Total Funding Pool: $2.5M+ Number of Grants: At least 5 organizations Funded projects should focus on building a sustainable pipeline of nonclinical mental health providers supporting young people. Who Are Nonclinical Mental Health Providers? Nonclinical providers are individuals who support youth mental health without providing formal clinical diagnosis or treatment . They play a critical role in expanding access to care through community-based, culturally relevant, and stigma-free support . Examples include: Community Health Workers Peer Support Specialists Care Navigators Health Educators These professionals: Provide guidance, mentorship, and emotional support Help youth navigate services Deliver prevention and education programmes Extend the reach of clinical systems Why This Funding Matters The youth mental health crisis is growing rapidly: Over 12 million young people experience mental health disorders annually Most lack access to adequate care 93% of behavioral health workers report burnout There is a critical shortage of licensed clinicians, making nonclinical providers essential to bridging the gap. However, these roles are often: Undervalued Underpaid Lacking clear career pathways This initiative aims to professionalize and strengthen this workforce while improving outcomes for young people. What Projects Will Be Funded? The Rare Impact Fund is looking for projects that strengthen the full workforce pipeline , including: 1. Attraction and Recruitment Bringing diverse individuals into mental health roles 2. Development and Training Building skills and competencies 3. Placement and Entry Supporting transition into real roles 4. Upskilling and Career Growth Providing advancement opportunities 5. Retention and Sustainability Ensuring long-term workforce stability Successful projects should be: Community-rooted Evidence-based Youth-centered Focused on workforce development (not direct service delivery alone) Eligibility Criteria To apply, organizations must: Be a registered nonprofit (501(c)(3) or equivalent) Operate in the United States Focus program activities within the U.S. Be non-political Submit only one application Request between $250,000 and $500,000 Organizations must also commit to: Annual cohort meetings Learning sessions (quarterly) Reporting and storytelling activities Priority Considerations Preference will be given to organizations that demonstrate: Experience supporting nonclinical mental health providers Strong focus on youth populations Workforce development expertise Integration of youth voice and lived experience Clear equity strategies targeting underserved communities Application Process The application follows a two-stage process : Stage 1: Letter of Intent (LOI) Applicants must submit: 1–2 page narrative Organizational background and experience Proposed program overview Workforce gap addressed Youth focus and equity strategy Apply here: https://forms.gle/jeP6kpsLMt6haw9m8 Stage 2: Full Proposal (By Invitation Only) Top 60 applicants will be invited to submit a detailed proposal including: Program design Theory of change Budget and sustainability plan Due diligence documentation Key Dates LOI Opens: March 9, 2026 Webinar: March 18, 2026 LOI Deadline: April 10, 2026 Full Proposal Invitations: May 11, 2026 Full Proposal Deadline: June 12, 2026 Grant Awards Announced: August 2026 Funding Distributed: September–October 2026 Informational Webinar An applicant webinar will be held to explain: Program goals Eligibility requirements Application process Organizations must register in advance to attend. A recording will be shared afterward. What Success Looks Like The initiative aims to achieve: Stronger career pathways for nonclinical providers Improved workforce retention Increased recognition of nonclinical roles Better youth mental health outcomes For the first time, the Rare Impact Fund is launching a public Request for Proposals , allocating more than $2.5 million in total – including a $500,000 investment from the Enlight Foundation – to strengthen the nonclinical youth mental health workforce. This new grant program will support nonprofits across the workforce talent pipeline, from recruitment and training to retention, with a focus on roles that support young people outside traditional clinical settings. This is a high-impact funding opportunity for organizations working at the intersection of: Youth development Mental health Workforce systems By strengthening the nonclinical workforce, this initiative has the potential to transform how young people access mental health support at scale. APPLY NOW Explore More Opportunities For more global grants, fellowships, and funding opportunities: Visit: https://opportunitiesforyouth.org/?s=grant The post Rare Impact Fund Request for Proposals (RFP) 2026: $2.5M+ Grants to Strengthen Youth Mental Health Workforce (Up to $500,000) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
